clues: This medication activates AMPK, a sensor that protects cells against various stresses
A historical precursor derived from the French lilac was used in medieval Europe to treat excessive urination
On the WHO list of Essential Medications, this was originally marketed in France under the name 'Glucophage'
Although treated differently now, this was originally treated with leeches as far back as 2600 BC

Termed the 'silent killer', nearly 50% of Amerian adults are affected by this disease
Although highly selective, this medication has a rare complication of intraoperative floppy iris syndrome during eye surgery
Included in the AGS Beers Criteria due to risk of falls, this medication is still commonly used in older adults
Because this medication relaxes the bladder neck, it can also cause retrograde ejaculation where semen enters the bladder
The development of this was inspired by research into Brazilian pit viper venom
Studied famously in the SOLVD, CONSENSUS, and ISIS-4 trials, this class of medications is among the most commonly prescribed worldwide
Buildup of bradykinin causes the most common side effect as well as the most dangerous
Discovered on accident in 1895, this is used extensively all over the world
Rontgen refused to patent his discovery, which helped rapid adoption around the world within a year
Risks from a single use are low, but cumulative exposure can damage living cells and DNA
